title: carlina canariensis var. canariensis
intro: Carlina canariensis var. canariensis, also known as the Canary Island thistle or Canary Islands thistle, is a biennial plant native to the Canary Islands, characterized by its prickly leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Carlina canariensis var. canariensis have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and soothing skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
